sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to fruits of commercial varieties of pomegranates grown from Punica granatum L., of the Punicaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging. Pomegranates for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to fruits of commercial varieties of pomegranates grown from Punica granatum L., of the Punicaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging. Pomegranates for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to fruits of commercial varieties of pomegranates grown from Punica granatum L., of the Punicaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging. Pomegranates for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
number=2
level=1

sections.title: MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS
CLASSIFICATION
sections.text: Pomegranates are classified in three classes as defined below:
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > CLASSIFICATION
number=2.2
level=2
Pomegranates are classified in three classes as defined below:

page_spans.text: Pomegranates are classified in three classes as defined below:

sections.text:
The pomegranates must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the varieties and to the area in which they are grown. The development and condition of the pomegranates must be such as to enable them:
­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS >

level=3

­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

page_spans.text:
The pomegranates must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the varieties and to the area in which they are grown. The development and condition of the pomegranates must be such as to enable them:
­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Pomegranates in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Pomegranates in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Pomegranate may be sized by count, diameter or weight in accordance with existing commercial trading practices. When such is the case, the package must be labelled accordingly.
A. When sized by count, size is determined by the number of individual fruit per package.
B. Pomegranate may be sized by diameter (the maximum diameter of the equatorial section of each fruit).
The following table is a guide and may be used on an optional basis.
Table A - Diameter
Size Code
Size Code
Diameter (mm)
1 A ≥81
2 B 71 – 80
3 C 61 -70
4 D 51 – 60
5 E 40 – 50
C. Pomegranate may be sized by weight (the individual weight of each fruit).
The following table is a guide and may be used on an optional basis.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
2 > 2.2 > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=2
Pomegranates in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:
- slight defects in shape;
- slight defects in coloring;
- slight skin defects including cracking.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the arils of the fruit.

sections.title: Class II
sections.retrieval_text:
2 > 2.2 > Class II
number=2.2.3
level=2
This class includes pomegranates which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the pomegranates retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation:
­ defects in shape;
­ defects in coloring;
­ skin defects including cracking.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the arils of the fruit.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
number=4
level=1
Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

page_spans.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
sections.text:
Pomegranates must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new 1, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue.
Pomegranates shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995).

Pomegranates must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new 1, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue.
Pomegranates shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995).

sections.title: SIZE TOLERANCES
sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number or weight of pomegranates corresponding to the size immediately above and/or below that indicated on the package.

sections.title: UNIFORMITY
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only pomegranates of the same origin, variety, quality and size (if sized). Sales packages may contain mixtures of varieties of different colors and sizes provided they are uniform in quality and for each variety concerned, its origin. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the pomegranates. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text:
7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CODEX STAN 193-1995).  
7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

page_spans.text:
7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CODEX STAN 193-1995).  
7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Code of Practice – General Principles of Food Hygiene  
(CAC/RCP 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. 
8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CAC/GL 21-1997).

pages.title: STANDARD FOR POMEGRANATE (CODEX STAN 310-2013)
pages.summary: A página apresenta o título principal do documento e um cabeçalho. Contém as seções 1 e 2 com seus respectivos subsídios numerados, seguindo a hierarquia normativa para pomegranates, incluindo requisitos e classificação. O mapeamento preserva a numeração, os títulos, e associa corretamente o texto interno, marcando continuidade para conteúdos que seguem em próximas páginas.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 310-2013
pages.summary: A página apresenta as subseções 2.2.2 Class I e 2.2.3 Class II da seção 2, com texto interno relativo às características e defeitos permitidos. Também contém a seção 3 PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING com explicação inicial e tabelas opcionais, marcando continuidade para a próxima página.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 310-2013
pages.summary: A página contém as seções 4 e 5 principais com suas subseções, incluindo textos explicativos para tolerâncias de qualidade e tamanho, e apresentação. A seção 5 continua na próxima página, indicada pela flag continues_on_next_page true para o nó 5.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 310-2013
pages.summary: A página contém as seções 5 e 6 principais com suas subseções e textos associados, com a seção 5 continuando da página anterior e continuando para a seguinte. A seção 6 começa nesta página.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 310-2013
pages.summary: A página contém as seções 7 e 8 principais, sem subdivisões, com seus textos respectivamente. Não há indicação de continuidade de conteúdo de ou para outras páginas nesta página.
